title: Belt-and-braces: per-iteration canonical-state coherence check — a cycle-state claiming a live phase for a cycle this loop is not running gets WARN+reconciled (covers SIGKILL/external-writer residue the RunCycle epilogue cannot)
summary: The cycle-1048 abnormal-exit epilogue (landed) guarantees seal+dossier on every RunCycle return path — but a SIGKILLed lane or external writer can still strand a stale in-flight record mid-batch. Add at the batch-iteration top (the blockerBreakerHalt chokepoint in cmd_loop.go): read canonical cycle-state; if unfinishedCycle(cs, last) is FALSE but cs.Phase names a live phase for a cycle number this loop is not currently dispatching, WARN loudly + mark phase=aborted (reuse the epilogue's state-floor semantics). Never touches a genuinely in-flight lane's state (per-run state files are separate; only the CANONICAL record is reconciled, and only between dispatches).
acceptance: RED: seeded stale in-flight canonical state + iteration top → WARN emitted + phase reconciled to aborted; a genuinely resumable unfinished cycle (boot-guard shape) is NOT touched
go test -race; wiring test at the cmd_loop call site
